name: Cloudflare WAF XXE Request Pattern Defense Pack
summary: Detects and blocks high-confidence XML External Entity (XXE) indicators in request bodies and parameters using conservative request pattern matching.
objective: Reduce XXE attack risk by identifying malicious XML DTD/entity directives and external entity retrieval patterns before XML parser processing.
risk_mitigated: Local file disclosure via XML entities
Server-side request forgery via XML parser external entities
Out-of-band data exfiltration through XML parser features

rules.description: Block likely XXE payload markers in request body/query/cookie with conservative XML-context gating and known XML-heavy endpoint exclusions.
rules.expression: (http.request.method in {"POST" "PUT" "PATCH"} and (lower(http.request.headers["content-type"][0]) contains "xml" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "<?xml") and (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "<!doctype" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "<!entity" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains " system " or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains " public " or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "file://" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "expect://" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "php://" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "gopher://" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "ftp://" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "jar:" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "xinclude" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "xml-stylesheet")) and not (http.request.uri.path starts_with "/soap/" or http.request.uri.path starts_with "/saml/" or http.request.uri.path starts_with "/xmlrpc" or http.request.uri.path starts_with "/api/xml/")
rules.notes: Default expression intentionally scopes to XML-like requests to reduce false positives in non-XML traffic. Endpoint exclusions are starter allowlist candidates and should be tailored per application inventory.

tuning_notes: Start with action=log (or simulate) for 7-14 days, then move to block after validating legitimate XML workflows.
Review alerts for SOAP/SAML/XML-RPC/API-XML endpoints and add precise path exclusions where business traffic includes benign DTD/entity constructs.
If attackers shift to encoded payloads, add additional decoded/normalized variants (URL-encoded and double-encoded indicators) in a staged rollout.
Prefer narrow exclusions (exact path + method + source) instead of broad global disables to preserve protection coverage.
deployment_notes: Deploy in log/monitor mode first to baseline normal XML parser traffic patterns.
Promote to block mode only after false-positive review with application owners for XML-heavy integrations.
Roll out progressively by zone/environment (dev -> stage -> production) and keep rollback ready via rule disable toggle.
Pair with secure parser configuration (DTD disabled, external entities disabled) for defense in depth.
monitoring_guidance: Track blocked/logged event counts by URI path, method, ASN, country, and user agent to identify abuse clusters.
Alert on sudden spikes in XXE marker hits, especially bursts against XML endpoints or authentication-adjacent APIs.
Review top matching substrings (DOCTYPE/ENTITY/SYSTEM/PUBLIC/file:// etc.) weekly to refine signal quality.
Correlate with origin logs and application parser errors to confirm attack activity versus legitimate integration noise.

false_positive_exclusions.guidance: Use these as initial exclusion candidates only when validated as business-required XML traffic. Keep exclusions as specific as possible and re-validate quarterly.
